prompt-2-iter-2: protagonist and main action positioned in the RIGHT 55% of the frame. The LEFT ~40% of the frame is where the story text sits, on cream paper beside the painting — so it must be the QUIETEST, most uncluttered part of the scene: open sky, distant landscape, soft atmospheric wash, room to breathe, with NO faces and NO key objects there. The LEFT edge of the illustration should DISSOLVE into the page like watercolor wash absorbing into paper — irregular, organic, wet-edge bleeding, not a clean rectangular boundary — so the painting melts toward the text rather than ending on a hard line. The painting should feel like it's spreading across the page, not pasted onto it.
prompt-3-iter-2:
The illustration will be placed as a wide watercolor BAND across the TOP of the page, with the story text printed on cream paper BELOW it. Paint a FULL watercolor scene that fills the frame edge to edge — do NOT leave any blank cream area inside the image, and do NOT paint any text or lettering.

PLACEMENT: keep the protagonist and the main focal action in the UPPER TWO-THIRDS of the frame, roughly centred. The BOTTOM third of the frame must be the calmest, quietest part of the scene — open ground, soft grass, calm water, gentle foreground wash, or atmospheric shadow — with no faces, no key action, and no important objects there, because the image's lower edge feathers softly into the cream paper of the page and the text sits just beneath it.

STYLE: intimate, tender, atmospheric watercolor with visible pigment granulation and soft organic edges — a quiet, contemplative moment.
prompt-6-iter-1:
The image fills a 1408×768 landscape frame. The composition will be cropped at render to a 1.29:1 aspect ratio (cropping ~15% from each horizontal edge). The painted scene must extend RICHLY to all four edges of the source frame — every corner of the source should contain meaningful painted content (sky, landscape, atmospheric elements, scene context). This is an edge-to-edge full-bleed composition with NO cream paper margins or empty borders.

The protagonist is positioned in the UPPER PORTION of the frame (approximately top 65% of the image) so the lower portion is available for text overlay. The subject's center of mass should sit at approximately 50% from the left edge and 30% from the top of the frame.

The LOWER ~30% of the frame is the TEXT-OVERLAY ZONE: the story text sits there behind a translucent cream panel, so this band must be the CALMEST, most UNIFORM part of the painting — open ground, calm water, sky-glow, soft atmospheric foreground — with LOW CONTRAST and NO fine texture, NO sharp edges, NO faces, and NO key objects, so the text reads cleanly through the panel. Keep all meaningful detail and focal content ABOVE this band.

This is the CLIMACTIC, most VISUALLY POWERFUL page of the whole book — the emotional peak. Compose it BIG and BOLD: a strong, large focal subject, a dynamic camera angle or a clear sense of scale, deep atmospheric perspective, and dramatic directional light, so this page feels markedly more immersive and cinematic than the calmer banded pages around it. The painted scene wraps around the viewer. Watercolor wash with rich pigment and atmospheric depth; the painting fills the page like a movie still — no margins, no whitespace, only the painted world. IMPORTANT: do not invent action or peril that isn't in the scene — if the peak moment is quiet or tender, deliver its power through SCALE, LIGHT, and ATMOSPHERE (a hushed, luminous, expansive frame), not forced drama.
prompt-7-iter-1:
Paint a FULL watercolor scene that fills the frame edge to edge — do NOT leave any blank cream area inside the image. Full-bleed watercolour reaching all four edges of the frame: no cream or white border, no vignette, no floating spot-illustration, no framed inset. The painting bleeds off every edge.

PLACEMENT: keep the protagonist and the main focal action in the UPPER TWO-THIRDS of the frame, roughly centred and generously sized. The BOTTOM THIRD of the frame is the calmest, least-detailed part of the composition — a soft, open wash such as quiet ground, calm water, or gentle foreground — with no faces, no key action, and no important objects there. This quiet lower band is purely a compositional choice for a restful, uncluttered foreground.

Do not paint, letter, or write any text, letters, words, captions, numbers, or signage anywhere in the image.

STYLE: warm, friendly, storybook watercolor with visible pigment granulation and soft organic edges, an inviting picture-book moment for a very young reader.
prompt-8-iter-1:
The image is a 3:4 PORTRAIT frame — taller than wide. This is a vertical composition. The painted scene fills the portrait frame edge-to-edge; compose to USE the full vertical scale of the frame.

VERTICAL COMPOSITION INTENT: This composition's expressive purpose is VERTICAL SCALE and reaching-upward energy. The composition should emphasize VERTICAL movement, scale, or gaze: the protagonist LOOKING UP, towering elements rising ABOVE the subject (vast sky, tall trees, rising stars, soaring clouds), or the subject framed against a TALL vertical backdrop. The UPPER portion of the frame is where the vertical scale unfolds — the towering sky, the rising elements, the vast above. The LOWER portion is where the protagonist is grounded.

SUBJECT PLACEMENT: protagonist centered horizontally in the portrait frame, positioned in the LOWER HALF of the frame (roughly bottom 40% — feet on the ground), looking UP or reaching UP toward the towering content above. Face clearly visible.

Full painted scene edge-to-edge — atmospheric watercolor wash extending to all four edges of the portrait frame. No cream margins, no contained vignette. Keep the protagonist and key action toward the LEFT and CENTER of the portrait; the RIGHT edge of the frame should be the calmest, most atmospheric part (soft sky, distant wash, negative space), because that edge feathers into the cream paper where the story text sits — let the painting melt toward the text rather than ending on a hard edge.
